title: Could You Survive a Zombie Apocalypse? (1 in 4 Don't Make It)
hook: Hour one, the outbreak reaches your street. By Year One, forty fenced acres is all you get to keep. Climb the real survival ladder behind the fiction — the grid failing on schedule, the water math, the checkpoint's brutal filter — and find out what's actually true about the scenario everyone jokes about.
body: POV: you're an ordinary suburban parent living the monotonic threat ladder of a zombie outbreak, HOUR 1 through YEAR 1. The premise is fiction — its real-world inspiration is rabies, a genuine bite-transmitted, near-100%-fatal-once-symptomatic pathogen. Everything built around it is real and sourced: the CDC's actual 2011 'Preparedness 101: Zombie Apocalypse' public health campaign; the real ~3-day just-in-time grocery supply chain behind FEMA's own '72-hour kit' guidance; Hurricane Rita's real 100-mile evacuation gridlock; the real 1-gallon-per-person-per-day water math; the real 1983 START 'black tag' mass-casualty triage protocol still used by U.S. EMS; the real Insurrection Act machinery that puts the National Guard at a checkpoint; and the episode's closing 'wait, that's real?' fact — a real 2009 peer-reviewed University of Ottawa paper that mathematically modeled a zombie outbreak and concluded only an immediate, overwhelming response prevents total collapse. A dramatized cautionary POV — the outbreak, the family, and the camp are fictional; the survival mechanics are real and sourced. Watch the whole series — every life, every level, every way the climb costs you. For educational and entertainment purposes.
pinned: The scariest fact in this episode is real: in 2009, University of Ottawa researchers actually built a math model of a zombie outbreak — and found that without an immediate, overwhelming response, the outbreak wins. Which stage do you think breaks the most people: the barricade, or the checkpoint?
poll: Which stage would break you first? HOUR 1 (the door) / WEEK 1 (the water math) / WEEK 3 (the bite you can't take back) / MONTH 2 (the fence's 1-in-4)

thumb.question: WHO MAKES IT TO YEAR ONE?
